description: New skills specific to project and program management roles. These roles heavily reuse existing cross-sector skills (project_management, stakeholder_management, cross_functional_collaboration, process_improvement, risk_management, communication, etc.). Only genuinely new skills that don't exist elsewhere in the library are added here.

skills.name: Program Management
skills.description: Coordinating multiple related projects or workstreams to achieve broader strategic outcomes. Distinct from project management in scope: program managers orchestrate dependencies across projects, manage program-level risks, align multiple delivery teams, and ensure the aggregate output meets strategic business objectives. Includes managing program budgets, P&L, resource allocation across projects, and executive-level reporting.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Supports coordination across 2-3 related projects. Tracks cross-project dependencies and escalates conflicts. Assists with program-level status reporting and milestone tracking.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages a program of 3-5 concurrent projects with cross-functional dependencies. Owns program timeline, budget, and risk register. Coordinates resource allocation across project teams. Delivers program-level status to senior leadership.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads complex, multi-domain programs spanning hardware, software, and operations. Manages program P&L and strategic alignment with business objectives. Navigates matrix organizations to drive execution across multiple teams. Presents program status and strategic decisions to C-suite and external stakeholders.

skills.name: Technical Project Delivery
skills.description: Bridging business requirements and technical implementation in project delivery. Includes understanding software development processes, API integrations, system architectures, and technical trade-offs well enough to translate business needs into actionable technical plans, manage engineering dependencies, and make informed scope decisions. Distinct from engineering in that the focus is on delivery orchestration rather than building.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic software development lifecycle and can communicate with engineering teams. Tracks technical tasks and dependencies with guidance. Familiar with tools like Jira for technical project tracking.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Translates business requirements into technical specifications and acceptance criteria. Manages engineering dependencies and technical risks independently. Understands API integrations, system architecture basics, and deployment processes. Serves as bridge between product/business stakeholders and engineering teams.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads technically complex, multi-system projects end-to-end. Makes informed trade-off decisions on scope, architecture, and timeline. Manages integration across hardware, software, and algorithmic components. Drives technical design reviews and system-level testing strategies.
